extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (p50), whose antecedent is the 8-way conjunction including diabetes. rec_drug_b is set true by rec_b_from_trigger (p50) whenever diabetes is true. Because diabetes is a conjunct required to fire rec_drug_a, any input that makes rec_drug_a true also satisfies the trigger for rec_drug_b. These rules target different outputs, so the per-output priority resolution never makes them compete — both fire independently, and no rule ever assigns rec_drug_a:=false or rec_drug_b:=false (defaults apply only to unset outputs, but both are set). Therefore the all-eight-true assignment yields rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously, violating mutual exclusion. ckd_dialysis is irrelevant (it only affects rec_drug_c) and is set false. Witness: diabetes, hypertension, peptic_ulcer, pregnancy, qt_prolongation, heart_failure, seizure_history, hepatic_impairment all true; ckd_dialysis false → rec_drug_a=true, rec_drug_b=true.
